quotes: Apply the palette in the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate
Headlines/headings: Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
Body text: Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds
Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.
**Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).

rules.intent: Maintain overall brand contrast and typography legibility rules across light and dark backgrounds.
rules.rule_text: Apply colors in the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio where blues and golds dominate. Apply Brand Blue (headline.color = #00529b) for headlines and Graphite (body.color = #212121) for body text on light backgrounds, switching to White (#ffffff) on dark backgrounds.

quotes: Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures
**Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.

rules.intent: Ensure visual hierarchy and clear emphasis of important metrics in charts.
rules.rule_text: In charts, Brand Blue (#00529b) and Sky Blue (#358CCB) must dominate; Sunshine (#faa31b)/Gold (#ffc60a) are strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures, and gray is used for placebo.

quotes: Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
**Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Maintain visual distinction between data highlights and editorial text blocks.
rules.rule_text: Callout surfaces must use Light Blue (#E6F0F9) at a 70% tint (opacity.callout.70 = 0.7) for data callouts, and Golden Sand (#FEF1C8) at a 30% tint (opacity.callout.golden_sand_box = 0.3) for text-heavy callouts.

effect.reserved_for: sparing tertiary accents only (never primary CTAs, headlines, or data)
effect.subject: color.tertiary.coral

quotes: sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data
**Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

rules.intent: Prevent the oversaturation of energetic tertiary colors, preserving brand credibility.
rules.rule_text: Coral (#F26A38) must only be used as a sparing tertiary accent and never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. Avoid Coral in email sections entirely unless explicitly requested by the brief.

quotes: Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Maintain consistent, high-contrast visual cues for interactive elements.
rules.rule_text: Links must be underlined (body.link.text_decoration = 'underline') and styled using Brand Blue (body.link.color = #00529b) on light backgrounds, and White (#ffffff) on dark backgrounds.

quotes: 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances
The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

rules.intent: Ensure trademark integrity and consistent logo brand rendering.
rules.rule_text: The logo icon's 70% transparency (opacity.logo.gold = 0.7) of Pantone 7548 C / Gold (#ffc60a) must be maintained in all instances, including both the standalone logo and when used as a background pattern or graphic design element.
